title: 10 best herbal creams for lice

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil (Melaleuca Alternifolia): Has potent insecticidal and antiseptic properties, which help to kill lice and their eggs, as well as soothe skin irritations caused by scratching.
Eucalyptus Oil (Eucalyptus Globulus): Possesses natural insect-repelling properties that help deter lice from attaching to the hair and scalp, making it more difficult for them to feed and multiply.
Lavender Oil (Lavandula Angustifolia): Exhibits calming and soothing properties that can reduce anxiety and stress related to lice infestations, as well as promote a healthy sleep-wake cycle.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with lice because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The essential oil found in tea tree cream has been shown to effectively kill lice and their eggs, also known as nits. The oil's active compounds, such as terpinen-4-ol, work to disrupt the lice's exoskeleton and prevent them from feeding and reproducing. As a result, tea tree cream has become a popular natural remedy for lice infestations, offering a safe and effective alternative to traditional chemical treatments.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): Helps kill lice and their eggs by disrupting the cuticle of the lice's exoskeleton, ultimately leading to their death.
Linalool: Has antiparasitic properties that help in killing lice by breaking down the cuticle of the lice's exoskeleton, which prevents them from feeding and reproducing.
Cymene: Helps to kill lice and their eggs by disrupting the cell membrane of the lice, causing their death.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high tea tree oil content, which is known for its insecticidal and antiparasitic properties against lice.
Stems: Utilized for their oil content, which is often combined with tea tree oil to enhance the effectiveness of lice creams.
Roots: Employed for their additional oil content, which is sometimes used as a substitute or in combination with other parts to create lice treatment creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of dried leaves in 1 liter of water for 20 minutes at 100c to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a heat-proof container to separate solid and liquid parts.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 50g of cocoa butter in a double boiler to melt the ingredients.
Gradually add 50g of the infused oil to the melted wax mixture while whisking to combine.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before scooping into containers for storage.

remedy_properties: Azadirachtin: This compound has been shown to be highly effective against lice eggs, nymphs, and adults by disrupting their life cycle and preventing re-infestation.
Salannin: Neem's salannin component has been found to have insecticidal properties that help kill lice and other parasites by damaging their nervous systems and reproductive organs.

Meliacins: The meliacins present in neem creams have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce the risk of infection caused by scratching or bites from lice.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica, also known as neem, creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties. The active compound, azadirachtin, repels and kills lice eggs and adult lice, effectively breaking the life cycle of the infestation. Neem creams also have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe irritated scalps and prevent secondary infections. This makes neem creams a popular and effective alternative to chemical-based treatments, providing a safe and natural solution for lice removal. Regular application can lead to complete eradication of the infestation.
remedy_constituents: Nimbin: Acts as a neurotoxin, disrupting the lice's nervous system and ultimately killing them.
Sesquiterpenoids: Interferes with the lice's ability to feed and reproduce, ultimately leading to their death.
Azadirachtin: Exhibits repellent properties, preventing lice from infesting the scalp and hair, and also has insecticidal effects that help kill existing lice.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make creams for lice due to the presence of neem oil, which has insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for lice due to the presence of azadirachtin, a compound that has insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties.
Seeds (also mentioned above): They are used to make creams for lice due to the presence of neem oil, which has insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties.
Seeds: They are used to make creams for lice due to the presence of neem oil, which has insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for lice due to the presence of azadirachtin, a compound that has insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties.
Fruits: They are not typically used, but the leaves and seeds are often used to make creams that can be applied to the scalp to treat lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest azadirachta indica leaves and flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Clean the azadirachta indica leaves and flowers thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 250 grams of azadirachta indica leaves and flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 2 hours and 30 minutes to allow for infusion of the herbs into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle for later use in cream preparation.

remedy_properties: Citral: Citral, a natural compound found in lemongrass oil, has been shown to be effective against lice eggs and adult lice due to its ability to disrupt their nervous system, ultimately leading to death.
Cineole: Cineole, another key component of lemongrass oil, exhibits antiparasitic properties that help repel and kill lice, making it an effective ingredient in herbal creams for lice treatment.

Terpenes: Terpenes, a class of compounds found in lemongrass essential oil, possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe itching and inflammation caused by lice bites, while also combating bacterial infections associated with lice infestations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties. The essential oils extracted from lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to repel and kill lice. These oils disrupt the lice's nervous system, causing them to become immobile and eventually die. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lemongrass creams may help soothe the scalp and reduce itching associated with lice infestations, making it a popular natural remedy for lice treatment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Limonene): This terpene has been shown to have insecticidal and antiparasitic properties, effectively killing lice and their eggs.
Geraniol: This terpene has been found to have a repellent effect on insects, including lice, helping to prevent infestations and reduce the spread of lice.
Limonene: This terpene has been demonstrated to have antiparasitic and antifungal properties, which can help eliminate lice and other microorganisms that may be contributing to an infestation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain oils that have antiseptic and antiparasitic properties, which can help in treating lice and other skin issues.
Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus contain oils similar to those found in the leaves, which can help in treating lice and other skin issues.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus contain oils that have antiseptic and antiparasitic properties, which can help in treating lice and other skin issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of cymbopogon citratus leaves, dried for 2 weeks to enhance their potency.
Infuse 20g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax, 100g of shea butter, and 200ml of infused oil in a double boiler.
Emulsify the mixture by whipping it with a hand mixer until it thickens and cools.

remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: A primary component of eucalyptus oil, eucalyptol has been shown to have pediculicidal activity against head lice and their eggs, helping to kill the parasites.
Terpinen-4-ol: Another key compound in Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil, terpinen-4-ol has been found to be effective in disrupting lice's nervous system, ultimately leading to their death.
Camphor: A natural insecticide present in eucalyptus oil, camphor helps to suffocate and repel lice, making it difficult for them to survive and thrive on the scalp.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with lice because of its potent antiparasitic properties. The main compound, eucalyptol, has been shown to repel and kill lice and their eggs. The cooling and decongestant properties of the cream also help to soothe the scalp, reducing itching and inflammation caused by the lice infestation. Regular application of eucalyptus globulus cream can help to eliminate lice and prevent re-infestation, providing a natural and effective solution for lice treatment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps in repelling and killing lice by creating an environment that's inhospitable for their survival and development.
α-Phellandrene: This terpene has insecticidal properties that help in killing lice, making it an effective constituent against lice infestations.
β-Pinene: This terpene has been found to have insecticidal and repellent properties, which can help in preventing lice from attaching to the scalp and reducing their numbers.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their potent insecticidal and antiparasitic properties, which effectively repel and kill lice.
Barks: Contain compounds that exhibit antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and treat lice infestations.
Essential oils extracted from Leaves: These oils, particularly eucalyptol, possess potent insecticidal and antiparasitic properties, making them a popular ingredient in lice creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a single layer on paper towels at room temperature for 2 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the eucalyptus globulus and coconut oil mixture in a warm water bath for 2 hours to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused coconut oil through a cheesecloth and whip it with 50 grams of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Basil contains a compound called eugenol, which has been shown to be effective against lice and their eggs by breaking down the outer shell of the louse, allowing for easier removal.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Basil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe skin irritation caused by lice bites and reduce swelling, making it a gentle and non-irritating treatment option.
Antioxidant properties: Basil's antioxidants can help protect the skin from oxidative stress caused by lice infestations, which can lead to premature aging and skin damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum, also known as basil, creams helps with lice because of its natural antiparasitic properties. The active compounds found in basil, such as eugenol and linalool, have been shown to repel and kill lice, thereby providing a safe and effective solution for those affected. These compounds disrupt the lice's nervous system, ultimately leading to their demise. Furthermore, basil creams also soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by lice infestations.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol, a phenolic compound found in basil, has been shown to have insecticidal and repellent properties, which can help in deterring lice from infesting the scalp or skin.
Linalool: Linalool, a terpene found in basil, has been reported to exhibit insecticidal activity, potentially helping to kill or repel lice.

Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid, a triterpenoid found in basil, has been found to have antiparasitic properties, which could contribute to the effectiveness of basil creams in treating lice infestations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing and eliminating lice.
Essential oil from Leaves: Extracted from the leaves, the essential oil is known for its insecticidal properties that help in repelling and killing lice.
Extract from Leaves: The extract from basil leaves is believed to have antiparasitic and antimicrobial properties, which aid in treating lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of fresh ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers early in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Steam distill the ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers in a ratio of 1:3 for 2 hours to extract essential oils.
Combine 50 grams of the extracted ocimum basilicum essential oils with 100 grams of a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of the ocimum basilicum-infused oil blend and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify for 1-2 hours before transferring the herbal ocimum basilicum cream into clean containers for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory property helps to soothe itchy scalps and reduce redness caused by lice bites, making it more comfortable for individuals to manage the condition.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold cream can help kill lice eggs (nits) and adult lice on contact, reducing the risk of re-infestation and promoting faster healing of affected areas.

Soothing and Calming: Pot marigold's soothing and calming effects can help reduce anxiety, stress, and discomfort associated with lice infestations, allowing individuals to manage their condition more effectively.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with lice because of its natural antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, including triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, can help break down the lice's exoskeleton, making it difficult for them to attach to the scalp. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming properties can reduce redness, itching, and irritation caused by the lice infestation, promoting a healthy scalp environment for the scalp to recover.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have been shown to have antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating lice infestations by reducing inflammation and killing lice and their eggs.

Naphthoquinones (e.g., carotenoids and quinones): These compounds have been found to exhibit antiparasitic and antibacterial activities, which can help in killing lice and preventing their re-infestation.

Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol, quercetin): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antiparasitic, and antioxidant properties, which can help in soothing skin irritation caused by lice infestations and reducing the risk of re-infestation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the skin and reduce irritation caused by lice.
Leaves: Leaves are often used in combination with flowers due to their ability to provide additional antiseptic and anti-inflammatory benefits, further aiding in the treatment of lice.

Seeds: Seeds are sometimes used in creams for lice due to their reported ability to repel lice and their potential to be a natural insecticide, although this use is less documented than the other two parts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers or 15 grams of dried flowers at peak bloom time.
Infuse 250 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 30 grams of fresh calendula flowers.
Combine 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 5 milliliters of infused calendula oil to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to a clean glass jar.

remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Rosemary's essential oils have been shown to repel and kill head lice, making it a natural and effective treatment for infestations. This is because the oils interfere with the lice's ability to breathe and feed.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm irritated skin caused by scratching or allergic reactions to lice bites, reducing redness and itching. This makes rosemary cream a gentle and comforting treatment option.

Antibacterial properties: Rosemary's antibacterial properties help prevent the growth of bacteria on the skin that can cause infection and further irritation, promoting a healthy and clean environment for healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with lice because of its natural antiparasitic and antiseptic properties. The essential oils present in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to repel and kill lice and their eggs. Additionally, rosemary has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the skin and reduce irritation caused by lice bites. By using a rosemary-based cream, individuals can effectively and naturally treat lice infestations without harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene acts as a potential insecticide, repelling and killing lice by disrupting their nervous system and causing oxidative stress.

Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties, which can help soothe skin irritation caused by lice bites and also repel or kill the parasites.
Cineole: A monoterpene with insecticidal properties, cineole can help to repel and kill lice by interfering with their nervous system and causing a rapid decline in their population.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly camphor and bornyl acetate, which have insecticidal properties.
Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are also used due to their content of essential oils, which help to repel and kill lice and their eggs.
Flowers: The flowers are used in some creams as they contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe the skin and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in early morning when they are at their peak aroma.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 2 hours to obtain 10ml of essential oil.
Combine 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler and melt them together for 10 minutes.
Add 10ml of the obtained essential oil and 5ml of distilled water to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Herbal sesame creams contain antiparasitic agents that help to kill lice eggs and adult lice, reducing infestation and eliminating the problem.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sesame cream's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness, itching, and discomfort associated with lice bites.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal sesame creams contain antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin and reducing the risk of infection or complications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum, also known as sesame, creams helps with lice because of its natural antiparasitic and insecticidal properties. The cream, rich in sesamin and sesamolin, has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including lice infestations. The active ingredients in the cream work by suffocating the lice, preventing them from feeding and laying eggs, ultimately leading to their death. This natural and non-toxic approach makes Sesamum indicum creams a popular choice for treating lice infestations, especially in children.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Sesamum indicum have shown to possess insecticidal properties, which can help in killing lice and their eggs.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Sesamum indicum have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help in soothing the skin irritation caused by lice infestation and preventing secondary infections.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Sesamum indicum have shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by lice infestation, promoting healing of the affected skin.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are used due to their oil content, which is often extracted and used in creams to repel and kill lice.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are used due to their antiparasitic properties, which help in treating and preventing lice infestations.
Seeds: Sesame seeds are also used due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help in preventing the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause infections related to lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of sesamum indicum flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal oil content and freshness.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Mix 100g of dried sesamum indicum flowers with 500g of carrier oil in a clean glass jar to create a 5:1 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every 2-3 days to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Peppermint oil has been shown to have antiparasitic effects, which helps to kill and repel lice, making it an effective ingredient in creams for treating lice infestations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol content in peppermint oil has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe the itching and irritation caused by lice bites, reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of relief.
Cooling and calming properties: Peppermint's cooling effect can provide temporary relief from the intense itching associated with lice infestations, allowing the skin to relax and reducing stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with lice because of its unique properties that create an environment inhospitable to lice. The cooling sensation and invigorating aroma of peppermint disrupt the lice's nervous system, causing them to become disoriented and eventually die. Additionally, peppermint's antiparasitic properties help to break down the lice's exoskeleton, making it difficult for them to survive. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective alternative to chemical-based treatments, offering a non-toxic solution to eradicate lice infestations.
remedy_constituents: Monoterpenes: These compounds have been shown to have insecticidal properties, which could help kill lice.
Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): This antioxidant has been reported to have insecticidal and repellent properties, potentially helping to deter or kill lice.
Menthone: This monoterpene has been reported to have insecticidal and antiparasitic properties, which could help combat lice infestations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol, which has natural insecticidal properties that help repel and kill lice.
Stems: Stems are used for their essential oils, which, like leaves, contain menthol and other compounds that aid in lice treatment and prevention.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, are used for their menthol content, which helps to kill and repel lice, and also for their soothing properties to calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh mint leaves in the morning when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested mint leaves in a low-temperature oven at 105-115°f for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Infuse 1 cup of dried mint leaves in 1 quart of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to separate the oil from solids.
Mix 10-20% of the infused oil with 80-90% of a natural emulsifier such as beeswax and candelilla wax to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's primary property is its ability to numb and anesthetize the scalp, making it uncomfortable for lice to feed on blood and reducing their populations.
Methyl salicylate: Methyl salicylate has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe itching and redness caused by lice bites, while also killing lice and their eggs.
Wintergreen essential oil: Wintergreen essential oil contains compounds like methyl salicylate and other terpenes that have natural insecticidal properties, helping to repel and kill lice.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties. The active compound methyl salicylate, found in Gaultheria procumbens, is a potent antiparasitic agent that effectively kills lice eggs and live lice. This herbal remedy has been traditionally used to repel and eliminate lice infestations, providing a safe and non-toxic alternative to chemical-based treatments. Its effectiveness in addressing lice issues makes it a popular choice among parents and healthcare professionals seeking natural solutions.
remedy_constituents: Gaultherin: Gaultherin is a sesquiterpene, a type of terpene, that has antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe the skin and reduce the discomfort caused by lice bites.

Phenolic acids: Gaultheria procumbens contains various phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that may help to inhibit the growth of lice and soothe the skin.

Ascorbic acid: Ascorbic acid, or vitamin C, is a potent antioxidant found in Gaultheria procumbens that may help to boost the immune system and promote the overall health of the skin, making it less susceptible to lice infestation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiparasitic properties to repel and kill lice.
Roots: Used for their antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the skin affected by lice.
Barks: Used for their antiseptic properties to prevent infection from lice bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in late summer or early fall.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the harvested leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of the steeped gaultheria procumbens liquid and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture thickens.
Strain the cooled cream through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with lice because of its potent insecticidal properties that effectively kill lice eggs, nymphs, and adult lice. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, penetrate the scalp and hair shaft, targeting the lice and their habitats. This non-toxic and gentle approach allows for a thorough eradication of lice infestations without damaging the skin or hair. Wormwood creams have been traditionally used to treat head lice and other parasitic infections, making them a natural and effective solution for lice removal.
remedy_properties: Artemisinin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties, which helps to reduce itching, swelling, and irritation caused by lice bites.
Thujone: Thujone has been shown to have ovicidal activity, meaning it can kill lice eggs, preventing re-infestation and helping to eliminate the infestation more quickly.
Essential oils: The essential oils present in wormwood creams, such as camphor and menthol, help to repel lice and soothe the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit insecticidal and larvicidal properties, making them effective against lice.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of thujone, a natural insecticide that helps repel and kill lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers for 20 minutes.
Combine 200 grams of dried artemisia absinthium with 400 milliliters of carrier oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to extract essential oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar within 15 minutes.
Mix equal parts of the infused oil and beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with lice because of its natural calming and soothing properties. Lavender oil, a key ingredient in these creams, has been shown to gently calm the skin and scalp, reducing itching and irritation caused by lice infestations. Additionally, the antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender oil help to combat the infection, making it difficult for lice to survive and multiply. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to treat lice without harsh chemicals or medicated shampoos.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic property: Lavender contains compounds like camphor, linalool, and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to repel and kill lice, helping to eliminate infestations.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with lice bites and allergic reactions.
Antiseptic property: Lavender's antiseptic properties help prevent secondary infections that can occur when the skin is scratched or bitten by lice, promoting a faster healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are effective in repelling and killing lice.
Flowers: Lavender flowers have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for soothing skin irritations caused by lice bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered lavender with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to allow the oils to infuse with the lavender properties.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool to room temperature before straining the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix in 10 grams of beeswax pellets and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil, then pour the mixture into sterilized containers and refrigerate until solidified.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Osage orange creams helps with lice because they contain a unique blend of natural oils that are toxic to these unwanted pests. The cream's active ingredients, including pyrethrum and rotenone, work together to suffocate the lice, killing them on contact while also soothing the scalp and reducing itchiness associated with infestation. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties help to moisturize the hair and scalp, promoting a healthy environment that is less conducive to re-infestation.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: These compounds have been shown to repel and kill lice, as they disrupt the insect's exoskeleton and prevent them from feeding on human blood.
Limonoids: Osage Orange contains limonoids, which are toxic to lice and other insects, helping to eliminate infestations by killing the parasites and preventing re-infestation.
Furanocoumarins: These compounds have been found to exhibit insecticidal and acaricidal properties, making them effective against lice and other ectoparasites that cause skin irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help emulsify and solubilize oils, making them effective against lice.
Barks: Contains tannins that aid in reducing inflammation and itching caused by lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried cornus mas bark, ensuring freshness and quality.
Combine 50 grams of the dried bark with 250 milliliters of boiling water, allowing steeping for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Mix 15 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler, melting at low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the herbal liquid with the melted wax-oil mixture, stirring until fully incorporated and thickened within 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil extracted from juniper berries contains compounds that disrupt the lice's exoskeleton, ultimately killing them. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to soothe the skin irritated by bites and prevent further infection. By using an herbal juniper cream, individuals can effectively eliminate lice infestations without relying on harsh chemicals or pesticides.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic activity: Juniper essential oil has been shown to effectively kill lice and their eggs, providing relief from infestation. This is due to its ability to disrupt the insect's nervous system.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Juniper cream can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with lice bites, making it a soothing and comforting treatment for affected areas.
Antimicrobial properties: Juniper oil has antimicrobial properties that help prevent bacterial infections caused by scratching or breaking of the skin due to lice infestation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties, making them effective in treating lice infestations.
Barks: The bark of the juniper tree contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial and antiparasitic activities, helping to eliminate lice and their eggs.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried juniper berries 10 grams and sweet almond oil 20 milliliters for preparation.
Infuse the dried juniper berries in boiling water 500 milliliters for 15 minutes steeping.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container discard solids.
Mix the strained liquid with melted beeswax 5 grams and vitamin e oil 2 milliliters stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers fill to the top and seal allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties, which target and kill these pesky parasites. The cream's active compounds, such as thymol, penetrate deep into the hair follicles and scalp, disrupting the life cycle of lice and their eggs. This gentle yet effective treatment not only eliminates existing infestations but also helps prevent re-infestation by killing any remaining lice or eggs that may have been missed during combing out.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to kill lice and their eggs by disrupting their cell membranes, thereby preventing further infestation.

Anti-Parasitic Properties: The volatile oils in thyme, particularly thymol, have been shown to be effective against head lice, killing them quickly and leaving no survivors, while also helping to soothe the skin and scalp.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Thyme's antioxidant properties help to reduce redness, itching, and inflammation caused by lice bites, while its anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids, minimize swelling and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in essential oils that help to repel and kill lice, providing effective relief from infestations.
Barks: The barks of the thyme plant contain antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin affected by lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the thymus vulgaris powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c for 30 minutes.
Add 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to the mixture and stir until melted and well combined, taking about 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify at room temperature, which may take approximately 60-90 minutes.
Once the cream has hardened, transfer it to an airtight container and store it in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months, with a shelf life of 2 years when refrigerated.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for lice are natural remedies containing plant extracts that help alleviate lice infestations by suffocating or repelling the parasites. The best herbal creams for lice include Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Lavandula angustifolia, Artemisia absinthium, Rosmarinus officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Cymbopogon citratus, Calendula officinalis, Teucrium chamaedrys, and Azadirachta indica. If left untreated, lice infestations can lead to stress, sleep deprivation, and social isolation, negatively impacting one's mental and emotional well-being, ultimately affecting daily life and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lice that are treated with herbal creams are scabies, ringworm, and cradle cap. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, lavender oil, and aloe vera can help soothe and calm the affected skin, reducing inflammation and itching associated with these conditions. They may also aid in killing fungal and parasitic infections that cause these ailments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with lice is a blend of tea tree oil, neem oil, and lavender oil. Tea tree oil has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the scalp and kill lice. Neem oil has natural insecticidal properties that repel and kill lice and their eggs. Lavender oil calms the scalp and reduces itching. Apply a mixture of equal parts tea tree oil and neem oil to the scalp, followed by a few drops of lavender oil. Leave it on for 30 minutes to an hour before washing.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Korean journal of parasitology, herbal creams derived from Pongamia pinnata leaves can be effective against lice due to the presence of pediculocidal and ovicidal properties in various extracts. The study, which utilized a filter paper diffusion method, found that petroleum ether extracts of P. pinnata leaves possessed excellent anti-lice activity, with values ranging between 50.3% and 100%. Furthermore, the study suggests that herbal creams made from P. pinnata leaves may be a viable alternative to synthetic anti-lice agents, which are increasingly facing resistance from head lice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with lice because of its unique properties. The active ingredient, tea tree oil, contains compounds that have potent insecticidal and antiparasitic effects. These compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, work to kill lice and their eggs, effectively breaking the lice life cycle. The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil also help to prevent the spread of infection caused by scratching and biting, making it an effective and natural solution for lice infestations.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This compound helps repel lice by disrupting their sensory perception and ability to detect their host.
Limonene: Limonene contributes to the antimicrobial and antifungal properties of Melaleuca alternifolia, which can help eliminate lice and their eggs.

Phenolic acids (mainly caffeic acid and ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in Melaleuca alternifolia have been shown to have potent antiparasitic and insecticidal properties, effectively killing lice and their eggs.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Melaleuca alternifolia for its antiseptic and insecticidal properties, which help in treating lice.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain essential oils that are effective against lice, making them a popular choice for lice creams.
Barks: The bark of Melaleuca alternifolia is also used for its antiseptic properties, which help in treating lice and other scalp infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steep the 100g of harvested plant material in 1 liter of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate solids from liquid.
Combine 100ml of the strained liquid with 200ml of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160-180°f for 15-20 minutes or until the mixture reaches a smooth consistent texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with lice because they contain the active compound eucalyptol, which has been shown to repel and kill lice. The cream's cooling and soothing properties also help to calm irritated scalps and alleviate itching caused by the lice infestation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptus globulus help to reduce redness and swelling associated with lice bites. Regular application of the cream can help to eliminate lice and prevent re-infestation.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps to repel lice and relieve itching due to its insecticidal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Caryophyllene: Acts as an anti-parasitic agent, helping to kill or repel lice and prevent their infestation.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits insecticidal properties that help to repel and kill lice, thereby preventing their re-infestation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of eucalyptol, a natural compound effective against lice and their eggs.
Barks: Used due to their rich content of salicylic acid and other compounds that help in treating lice and soothing irritated skin.
Stems: Used due to their essential oils, particularly eucalyptol, which help in repelling and killing lice.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250 grams of eucalyptus globulus leaves in 2 liters of carrier oil at 50-60°c for 4-6 hours.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Blend 200 grams of the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the blended mixture until it cools and thickens to a desired consistency.
Package the cooled and thickened cream into airtight containers for storage and use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with lice because they contain natural compounds that repel and kill lice. The antiparasitic and antiseptic properties of lavender oil in these creams disrupt the lice's life cycle and prevent them from reproducing. Additionally, the soothing properties of lavender oil can help to reduce irritation and itching caused by the lice. Regular application of these creams can lead to the elimination of lice infestations, providing a natural and effective solution for those affected. This makes lavender creams a popular choice for lice treatment.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an insecticide, repelling and killing lice due to its toxicity towards these external parasites.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to the antiparasitic and antimicrobial properties of Lavandula angustifolia, helping to eliminate lice and prevent their re-infestation.
Limonene: Exhibits insecticidal and repellent properties, making it an effective natural ingredient in preventing lice infestations and treating existing cases.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the essential oils that help repel and kill lice.
Flowers: They are a primary source of the essential oil, lavender oil, which has insecticidal and antiparasitic properties.
Seeds: They contain compounds that can help in the extraction of essential oils used in lice treatment creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of their fragrance and potency during the summer months.
Steep the dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain 1 cup of lavender infused oil.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets with 1 cup of lavender infused oil in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Whip the cooled mixture with a handheld electric mixer until it thickens to a creamy texture suitable for application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium creams helps with lice because of its potent insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties. The active compounds in the plant, including thujone and absinthin, have been shown to effectively kill lice and their eggs. The creams can penetrate deep into the hair and scalp, targeting the lice and disrupting their life cycle. As a result, Artemisia absinthium creams have been used as a natural treatment for lice infestations, providing a safe and effective alternative to chemical-based treatments.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Exhibits insecticidal properties, making it effective in killing lice by disrupting their nervous system.
Absinthin: Has been shown to possess antiparasitic properties, which help in eliminating lice and their eggs from the scalp.
Bornyl acetate: Displays insecticidal and antifungal properties, contributing to the overall efficacy of Artemisia absinthium in treating lice infestations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain thujone, which has antiparasitic properties that help combat lice infestations.
Flowers: They are a source of sesquiterpene lactones, which possess anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties useful in treating lice.
Stems: They contain volatile oils, including camphor and thujone, that can help repel and kill lice.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids immediately.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of the herbal infusion and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the saucepan mixture.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties, derived from the essential oils of the rosemary plant. The camphor and borneol compounds in rosemary oil are known to repel and kill lice and their eggs, providing relief from infestations. The anti-inflammatory properties of rosemary oil also help soothe itchy scalps and skin irritated by lice bites, promoting overall scalp health and reducing the risk of future infestations. This natural approach can be a valuable addition to traditional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has shown potential in treating lice by disrupting the insects' nervous system, ultimately leading to their death.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, rosmarinic acid can help to eliminate lice by inhibiting their growth and development, as well as reducing the severity of lice bites.
Bornyl acetate: A monoterpene with insecticidal properties, bornyl acetate can help to repel or kill lice by disrupting their nervous system and preventing them from feeding on human blood.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly camphor and bornyl acetate, which have anti-parasitic properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Rosmarinus officinalis are also used as they contain a higher concentration of camphor and other essential oils that help in repelling and killing lice.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove plant material.
Add a natural emulsifier like beeswax to the infused oil in a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Melt the beeswax and infused oil mixture in a double boiler under low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with lice because of its soothing and antiparasitic properties. The gel extract from the Aloe vera plant is rich in compounds that repel and kill lice, thereby preventing infestation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce irritation and itching associated with lice bites. Additionally, its moisturizing properties help to soothe the scalp, making it an effective remedy for lice control without the use of harsh chemicals, promoting a healthy scalp and hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have been shown to have anti-parasitic properties, helping to repel and kill lice by disrupting their life cycle.
Polysaccharides: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, reducing the discomfort and itchiness caused by lice infestations, making them more manageable.
Glucomannans: These compounds have been found to have antiparasitic and antiseptic properties, helping to eliminate lice and their eggs, and preventing the spread of infestation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high gel content, which is effective in soothing the scalp and helping to remove lice and their eggs.
Seeds: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties, which help to combat lice infestations.
Barks: They contain compounds that have antiseptic and antiparasitic properties, which help to prevent the spread of lice and other scalp infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 5-7 leaves of aloe barbadensis gel in a sterile environment.
Weigh and mix 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel with 50 grams of distilled water.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of candelilla wax to the mixture and melt.
Stir in 5 grams of sweet almond oil and 2 grams of vitamin e oil into the cooled mixture.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal properties, which are derived from the essential oils of the lemongrass plant. These oils, particularly citronella and geraniol, possess potent antiparasitic and repellent properties that can effectively kill lice and their eggs. The creams work by penetrating the hair and scalp, where they target and eliminate the lice, preventing further infestation and promoting a healthy scalp environment. This makes Cymbopogon citratus creams a popular and effective natural remedy for lice infestations.
remedy_constituents: Citral: This monoterpene has anti-inflammatory and antiparasitic properties that help in treating lice infestations by reducing itching and killing the parasites.
Limonene: This monoterpene has insecticidal and antiparasitic properties that help in killing lice and preventing their re-infestation by disrupting their life cycle.
Geraniol: This monoterpenol has antiparasitic properties that help in killing lice and their eggs by inhibiting their growth and development, thereby reducing the infestation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Due to their high citral content, which is known for its insecticidal properties.
Rhyzomes: They contain oils with insecticidal properties, which help in treating lice infestations.
Stems: Similar to leaves and rhyzomes, stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used due to their citral content and other essential oils that help in treating lice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 bunch of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from your garden or purchase from local market.
Steam 100g of the harvested leaves for 10 minutes to release their essential oils.
Mix 50g of coconut oil with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the infused oil from step 2 with 10g of vitamin e oil and mix thoroughly.
Pour the blended mixture into 100g containers and allow to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with lice because of its antiparasitic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, are known to disrupt the lice's life cycle, preventing them from reproducing and eventually leading to their elimination. Additionally, Calendula's soothing and moisturizing properties help to alleviate the discomfort and itching caused by lice infestations, making it a gentle and effective treatment option for both children and adults. This natural remedy promotes overall scalp health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties that help soothe itching and reduce irritation caused by lice infestation.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help kill or inhibit the growth of lice and their eggs, ultimately reducing their population.

Carotenoids (including Calendulin): They have antioxidant properties and can help protect the skin from damage caused by lice bites and scratching, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in treating lice infestations.
Leaves: Their antimicrobial properties help in controlling lice growth and reducing the risk of re-infestation.
Seeds: They contain compounds that have been shown to repel lice and other parasites, making them a valuable ingredient in lice creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of dried calendula flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Melt 50g of beeswax in a double boiler and mix with 150ml of the calendula infused oil.
Add 10ml of vitamin e oil and 20 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and allow to cool and set for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Teucrium chamaedrys creams helps with lice because it contains the natural compound labdane, which has been shown to have potent insecticidal properties. The cream's active ingredients work by disrupting the lice's nervous system, ultimately leading to their death. This natural approach can be a gentle and effective solution for treating lice infestations, especially in individuals who may be sensitive to harsh chemicals or prefer a more holistic remedy. Studies have demonstrated its efficacy in eliminating lice and their eggs, providing relief from the discomfort and embarrassment associated with lice infestations.
remedy_constituents: Volatile oils: These oils help repel lice and their eggs, providing temporary protection against infestations.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antiparasitic properties that help kill lice and their eggs, reducing the risk of infestation.
Rosmarinlic acid (a phenolic compound): This compound has insecticidal properties that help kill lice and their eggs, providing relief from infestations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their antiparasitic properties, which help in repelling and killing lice.
Stems: Stems are used because they contain compounds that have been shown to have insecticidal and antiparasitic effects.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their antiparasitic and antibacterial properties, which aid in treating lice infestations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh teucrium chamaedrys leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Infuse 20g of dried teucrium chamaedrys in 100ml of boiling water for 5 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until fully melted.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with 20g of aloe vera gel and the melted wax and oil mixture to create the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica creams helps with lice because of its natural insecticidal and repellent properties. The active compound, nimbin, in the cream disrupts the lice's life cycle, preventing them from reproducing and eventually killing them. The cream also helps to loosen and remove the lice's eggs and dead bodies from the hair. This makes it an effective and non-toxic treatment for lice infestations. Regular use of Azadirachta indica cream can help to prevent re-infestation and promote a healthy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: They help with lice by disrupting the life cycle of lice and preventing them from reproducing, ultimately leading to their death.
Phenolics (Azadirachtin): They help with lice by inhibiting the growth and development of lice, as well as causing them to die, due to their toxic and insecticidal properties.
Flavonoids (Quercetin): They help with lice by reducing inflammation and itching associated with lice infestations, providing relief to the affected individual.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make creams for lice due to their high content of neem oil, which is known for its insecticidal and anti-parasitic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for lice as they contain azadirachtin, a compound that is toxic to lice and other insects.
Seeds (Again): They are also used to make creams for lice due to their ability to repel and kill lice, and to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried azadirachta indica leaves in a 500ml solvent like ethanol and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and collect the liquid extract discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.
Add 50 grams of the azadirachta indica extract to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture stirring constantly.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
